rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target component type or implementation scope based on whether constraints refer to abstract interfaces or concrete bindings.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structure by only referencing visible features or explicitly declared variables in AGREE contracts.
Place output range guarantees in component type annexes when bounds are concrete and features are visible; defer implementation-specific assignments to implementation annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in annex agree {** ... **}; and use eq/const/assume/guarantee declarations consistently.
Comparison expressions producing boolean results should be declared with eq : bool = ...;
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables—only use explicitly exposed ports or constants.
Avoid using subcomponent paths or implementation-only names in component type annexes.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE annex placement strategy for current target
